fields.summary: Implement a RMStateStore cleaner for deleting application/attempt info
[Umbrella] RM Restart Phase 1: State storage and non-work-preserving recovery

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: Now that we are storing the final state of application/attempt instead of removing application/attempt info on application/attempt completion(YARN-891), we need a separate RMStateStore cleaner for cleaning the application/attempt state.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Check time cost for recovering max-app-limit applications

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

comments.body:
Upload a patch:
- create a separate removeAppdispatcher in RMStateStore for handing app removal requests. These app removal requests will be processed once the configured retain time is met.
- The app removal requests will be added to the removeAppDispatcher once application is completed,

  http://issues.apache.org/jira/secure/attachment/12611527/YARN-1378.1.patch
  against trunk revision .

This message is automatically generated.

Hi Jing, thank you for taking this JIRA. Now we are taking ZKRMRStateStore-related issues such as YARN-1026, YARN-1222, YARN-1307 and so on. My concern is whether this JIRA blocks them or is blocked by them.
Hi [~ozawa], this jira is oriented only for periodically cleaning app/attempt data in state store, should not block or blocked by them, but may need code level rebase

Reviewed the patch. Mostly looks good, some comments:
 - RMStateStore.RemoveAppEventHandler: The time-based removal of apps from state-store causes a memory issue - we have to remember 2hrs worth of events *in addition* to what RMAppManager is remembering. We should try to solve these together?
- Once you do the above, RMStateStoreEvent doesn't need a timestamp.
 - RMStateStoreTestBase: ConverterUtils.toApplicationId? Use ApplicationId.newInstance.
 - Write a new test to make sure that an app which wasn't deleted from state-store by previous RM but has successfully completed is removed after RM restart.

New patch:
- Remove app from state store when the application is kicked out from RMContext.RMApp list. This makes apps remembered by state store (on disk) consistent with apps remembered by RM (in memory).
- Rename removeApplicationState to removeApplicationStateInternal
- Fixed other comments also.

This message is automatically generated.

Patch mostly looks good. We may need to revisit it depending on how much time RM recovery needs for recovering 10K finished apps. Can you check that and file a separate ticket?

Regarding the test, we can change it a little - Set apps limit to be 1, run an app that finishes, restart RM, makes sure the previous RM exists in memory as well as the state-store, then submit one more app at which point the first app should disappear both the RM context as well as the store.
New patch changed the test case accordingly

I would like us to measure before committing to this change. If the time taken to load so much state is too large then the approach taken by the current solution would be a non-starter right? Doesnt harm in measuring before committing the change since this patch isnt really blocking anything, right?

This message is automatically generated.

Did the experiment with ZK in my local machine, storing 5000 application state data takes about 13- 14 secs, and loading 5000 applications state data from ZK takes 2-3 secs. This does not include any attempt data.
We can introduce one more configuration for limiting the number of completed applications stored in disk if limited.

Do you think loading application attempts will have cost proportional to apps since there will be at least 1 app attempt per app? Would that double the numbers we are seeing in the experiment? (which would be true in case the cost is proportional to the number of znodes) What about all the delegations tokens that we will be recovering? ZK on a real cluster will be on a different remote machine which might be slower to access. How about other state store impls? ZK is probably the fastest for such retrievals. We recommend using ZK store but folks may use HDFS store.

- Add a new configuration for controlling the number of completed apps kept in state store, by default, it equals to max_completed_apps in RM memory
- Fix some redundant loggings when loading RM state.
- Fixed a bug in RMAppImpl.RMAppRecoveredTransition that as long as the final state of the app is available, return the final state of the app.
- changed zk delete ops to use version -1 to match all versions
- remove RMAppRemovedEvent and associated stuff

Tested on single node cluster with 10000 finished apps (each app includes one attempt),
 HDFS store takes about 16.5 secs to recover all applications and ZooKeeper takes about 8.5 secs.

bq. Fixed a bug in RMAppImpl.RMAppRecoveredTransition that as long as the final state of the app is available, return the final state of the app.
Can you add a test validating the fixing of this bug? Two things we can add to TestRMRestart
 - Start an app and kill it immediately before it starts any attempt. Recover it and make sure it doesn't have any attempts.
 - I believe this test exists, but please verify. Start an app and two/three appattempts.
    -- Fail the RM before the final app-state persistence and validate that the app doesn't get rerun again
    -- RM crashes after the final app-state perisstence and the app doesn't rerun again.

Added the test case.

bq. I believe this test exists, but please verify.
verified this test scenario exits.

comments.body:
Upload a patch:
- create a separate removeAppdispatcher in RMStateStore for handing app removal requests. These app removal requests will be processed once the configured retain time is met.
- The app removal requests will be added to the removeAppDispatcher once application is completed,

This message is automatically generated.

Hi Jing, thank you for taking this JIRA. Now we are taking ZKRMRStateStore-related issues such as YARN-1026, YARN-1222, YARN-1307 and so on. My concern is whether this JIRA blocks them or is blocked by them.
Hi [~ozawa], this jira is oriented only for periodically cleaning app/attempt data in state store, should not block or blocked by them, but may need code level rebase

Reviewed the patch. Mostly looks good, some comments:
 - RMStateStore.RemoveAppEventHandler: The time-based removal of apps from state-store causes a memory issue - we have to remember 2hrs worth of events *in addition* to what RMAppManager is remembering. We should try to solve these together?
- Once you do the above, RMStateStoreEvent doesn't need a timestamp.
 - RMStateStoreTestBase: ConverterUtils.toApplicationId? Use ApplicationId.newInstance.
 - Write a new test to make sure that an app which wasn't deleted from state-store by previous RM but has successfully completed is removed after RM restart.

New patch:
- Remove app from state store when the application is kicked out from RMContext.RMApp list. This makes apps remembered by state store (on disk) consistent with apps remembered by RM (in memory).
- Rename removeApplicationState to removeApplicationStateInternal
- Fixed other comments also.

This message is automatically generated.

Patch mostly looks good. We may need to revisit it depending on how much time RM recovery needs for recovering 10K finished apps. Can you check that and file a separate ticket?

Regarding the test, we can change it a little - Set apps limit to be 1, run an app that finishes, restart RM, makes sure the previous RM exists in memory as well as the state-store, then submit one more app at which point the first app should disappear both the RM context as well as the store.
New patch changed the test case accordingly

I would like us to measure before committing to this change. If the time taken to load so much state is too large then the approach taken by the current solution would be a non-starter right? Doesnt harm in measuring before committing the change since this patch isnt really blocking anything, right?

This message is automatically generated.

Did the experiment with ZK in my local machine, storing 5000 application state data takes about 13- 14 secs, and loading 5000 applications state data from ZK takes 2-3 secs. This does not include any attempt data.
We can introduce one more configuration for limiting the number of completed applications stored in disk if limited.

Do you think loading application attempts will have cost proportional to apps since there will be at least 1 app attempt per app? Would that double the numbers we are seeing in the experiment? (which would be true in case the cost is proportional to the number of znodes) What about all the delegations tokens that we will be recovering? ZK on a real cluster will be on a different remote machine which might be slower to access. How about other state store impls? ZK is probably the fastest for such retrievals. We recommend using ZK store but folks may use HDFS store.

- Add a new configuration for controlling the number of completed apps kept in state store, by default, it equals to max_completed_apps in RM memory
- Fix some redundant loggings when loading RM state.
- Fixed a bug in RMAppImpl.RMAppRecoveredTransition that as long as the final state of the app is available, return the final state of the app.
- changed zk delete ops to use version -1 to match all versions
- remove RMAppRemovedEvent and associated stuff

Tested on single node cluster with 10000 finished apps (each app includes one attempt),
 HDFS store takes about 16.5 secs to recover all applications and ZooKeeper takes about 8.5 secs.

bq. Fixed a bug in RMAppImpl.RMAppRecoveredTransition that as long as the final state of the app is available, return the final state of the app.
Can you add a test validating the fixing of this bug? Two things we can add to TestRMRestart
 - Start an app and kill it immediately before it starts any attempt. Recover it and make sure it doesn't have any attempts.
 - I believe this test exists, but please verify. Start an app and two/three appattempts.
    -- Fail the RM before the final app-state persistence and validate that the app doesn't get rerun again
    -- RM crashes after the final app-state perisstence and the app doesn't rerun again.

Added the test case.

bq. I believe this test exists, but please verify.
verified this test scenario exits.
